titleEn: REDU 2025 Conference held at the University of Granada
excerptEn: The University of Granada hosted the REDU 2025 Conference on 13 and 14 November, a national gathering focused on the role played by Academic Training and Development Units within the university system. Under the motto The Academic Training and Development Units: what do we influence? An analysis of strategies, programmes and practices…
contentEn:
The University of Granada hosted the REDU 2025 Conference on 13 and 14 November, a national gathering focused on the role played by Academic Training and Development Units within the university system. Under the motto The Academic Training and Development Units: what do we influence? An analysis of strategies, programmes and practices that leave their mark, the event brought together representatives of different institutions to share experiences, good practice and lines of teaching innovation.

The opening session was held in the Main Lecture Hall of the Higher Technical School of Architecture, and featured addresses by academic authorities of the University of Granada and of the University Teaching Network (REDU).

The IUCE was present at the conference through the participation of Dr M. José Rodriguez Conde, who attended, helping to strengthen collaboration with other university training units and raising the profile of the work carried out at the Institute in the areas of teaching support and educational improvement.

titleEn: 1st Interdisciplinary Research Seminar of the University Institute of Education Sciences
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca is holding, on 13 and 14 November, the 1st Interdisciplinary Research Seminar: Challenges and Future Prospects, a meeting place for sharing progress, building connections between groups and mapping out new lines of work within the Institute. The programme…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca is holding, on 13 and 14 November, the 1st Interdisciplinary Research Seminar: Challenges and Future Prospects, a meeting place for sharing progress, building connections between groups and mapping out new lines of work within the Institute.

The programme opens with a welcome from Susana Olmos Migueláñez, director of the IUCE, followed by a presentation of the research lines of the Institute's recognised research groups (GIRs), setting out the research projects in which they are involved, their methodological approaches, active initiatives and opportunities for collaboration among the different groups that make up the Institute.

The sessions explore a variety of fields of knowledge and how they interrelate, such as interaction in e-learning, discourse analysis, digital literacy, medical visualisation, teaching innovation, the study of educational heritage, physical activity, artificial intelligence applied to education, and processes of inclusion and diversity. There are also papers sharing experiences, results and emerging proposals relating to educational technology, digital storytelling, sustainability, pedagogical models and new trends in educational research.

The seminar also includes invited talks addressing the challenges of contemporary education, as well as the exploration of interdisciplinary synergies and possible joint projects to strengthen the IUCE's role as a benchmark in educational research, reinforcing the institution's commitment to innovation, quality and the advancement of knowledge in the field of education.

Access the full programme

titleEn: María José Rodríguez Conde presents her candidacy for the Presidency of the Spanish Pedagogy Society
excerptEn: Professor María José Rodríguez Conde, Professor of Research Methods and Diagnosis in Education at the University of Salamanca and a researcher affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), presents her candidacy for the Presidency of the Spanish Pedagogy Society (SEP). As the SEP has announced, following the review…
contentEn:
Professor María José Rodríguez Conde, Professor of Research Methods and Diagnosis in Education at the University of Salamanca and a researcher affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), presents her candidacy for the Presidency of the Spanish Pedagogy Society (SEP).

As the SEP has announced, following the review of the applications submitted, the approved candidacies are:

President: María José Rodríguez Conde

Second Vice-President: Coral González Barbera

Deputy Secretary: Ana María Novella Cámara

Treasurer: Fernando Martínez Abad

Professor Rodríguez Conde puts forward a project committed to the continuity and renewal of the SEP, the promotion of educational research and its transfer, the strengthening of cooperation with other national and international bodies and networks, and teamwork based on transparency, participation and public service to education.

SEP members are encouraged to exercise their right to vote, whether in person, by proxy or by post. In the latter case, only votes received before 2 pm on 18 November will be accepted.

All the information about the electoral process and the candidacy is available on the SEP website:

titleEn: The Doctoral Week brings together the programme's doctoral candidates
excerptEn: Seminars and presentations of research progress at the IUCE.
contentEn:
The Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme held its annual Doctoral Week at the IUCE's headquarters in the Solís Building.

Over the course of a week, the doctoral candidates presented the progress of their research to the programme's groups, in sessions combined with methodological seminars and networking activities for researchers.

excerptEn: The TEEM’25 International Conference (Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality) was held from 21 to 24 October 2025, organised by the GRIAL Research Group (Interaction and eLearning Research Group) in collaboration with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca. Throughout the week, researchers, teachers…
contentEn:
The TEEM’25 International Conference (Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality) was held from 21 to 24 October 2025, organised by the GRIAL Research Group (Interaction and eLearning Research Group) in collaboration with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca.

Throughout the week, researchers, teachers and professionals from the international educational and technological arena shared experiences, advances and reflections on the role of innovation and technology in the educational ecosystems of the present and the future.

Alongside the academic papers, there were networking spaces where participants were able to debate, exchange ideas and explore future collaborations between research groups.

The event featured keynote talks, round tables and papers addressing topics related to artificial intelligence in education, digital teacher training, educational inclusion and the design of open, collaborative learning environments, consolidating TEEM as a space for international exchange and dialogue.

It took place in an atmosphere of collaboration and shared learning, underlining the commitment to cutting-edge educational research and the transfer of knowledge to society.

The IUCE would like to thank all the attendees, speakers and collaborators whose involvement made this edition possible.
See you again at TEEM’26.

Further information is available on the conference's official website: teemconference.eu.

titleEn: Call for proposals now open for the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference (JIDUCYL25)
excerptEn: The inter-university group formed by the Teaching Training and Innovation areas of the public universities of Castilla y León (FIUniCyL) is organising the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference (JIDUCYL25), which will be held in a hybrid format (a first phase online and a second phase on site). On behalf of the University of Salamanca, the University Institute of Education Sciences…
contentEn:
The inter-university group formed by the Teaching Training and Innovation areas of the public universities of Castilla y León (FIUniCyL) is organising the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference (JIDUCYL25), which will be held in a hybrid format (a first phase online and a second phase on site). On behalf of the University of Salamanca, the University Institute of Education Sciences, which manages the Teaching Staff Training Plan, is collaborating in the organisation of the event.

Following the Valladolid and León editions, the conference has established itself as a meeting point for fostering and sharing experiences and good practice among teaching staff at the public universities of Castilla y León. This academic year, the event will take place on Friday 13 March 2025 in Burgos, with a preliminary phase held online. All teaching and research staff (PDI) of the public universities of Castilla y León may attend and take part in the conference.

The deadline for submitting proposals is 1 December 2025 and the thematic strands selected for participation in the 3rd JIDUCyL are as follows:

1.     Evidence-based innovation and the improvement of university teaching

2.     Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies in higher education

3.     Active, student-centred methodologies

4.     Innovative assessment and lifelong learning

5.     Inclusion, diversity and equity in university education

6.     Change management and a culture of innovation at the university

Further information on the conference website.

titleEn: Real and imagined challenges of higher education in Latin America
excerptEn: During his short stay at the IUCE, Professor Galileo Violini, Doctor Honoris Causa of Ricardo Palma University (Lima, Peru) and consultant to organisations such as UNESCO, the Government of Guatemala and the Government of the Dominican Republic, delivered, on Monday 6 October, in the Degree Hall of the Faculty of Education (Solís Building), the…
contentEn:
During his short stay at the IUCE, Professor Galileo Violini, Doctor Honoris Causa of Ricardo Palma University (Lima, Peru) and consultant to organisations such as UNESCO, the Government of Guatemala and the Government of the Dominican Republic, delivered, on Monday 6 October, in the Degree Hall of the Faculty of Education (Solís Building), the lecture entitled:

Real and imagined challenges of higher education in Latin America. Higher education in Latin America faces both real and imagined challenges. Among the real ones are insufficient funding, social inequality, weak links between science and innovation, and ministerial bureaucratisation that limits university autonomy. Added to this is the lack of sustained science policies and of effective regional cooperation. The imagined challenges stem from simplistic narratives: universities subordinated to political or market circumstances, science seen as a luxury, the false dichotomy between mass access and excellence, or a focus on formal aspects. The current scientific crisis in Latin America nevertheless opens up historic opportunities to strengthen home-grown capacities, promote science diplomacy and consolidate higher education as a driver of sustainable development, regional integration and democracy in Latin America. Europe and Spain have two options for taking part in this process: Neocolonialism or Multilateral Cooperation.

This activity, organised by the GRIAL Research Group and the GRIALE2 Knowledge Transfer Group, forms part of the development of the projects: Assessment and analysis of educational equity with large-scale assessments (ONSIDE): development of a web application for its promotion (PID2024-156106OB-I00) and Assessment of the quality of student-centred learning environments at university: transfer towards teacher training and academic guidance (PID2024-156950NB-I00).

titleEn: 1st Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with AI
excerptEn: The 1st Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with AI of the University of Salamanca (USAL) exceeded expectations on 26 September 2025, bringing together nearly 200 lecturers in the classrooms of the IUCE and the Faculty of Education. This great interest underlines the USAL's priority of meeting the demands of teaching staff…
contentEn:
The 1st Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with AI of the University of Salamanca (USAL) exceeded expectations on 26 September 2025, bringing together nearly 200 lecturers in the classrooms of the IUCE and the Faculty of Education.

This great interest underlines the USAL's priority of meeting the demands of teaching staff through a range of training actions. The attendees, divided into four working groups, shared and discussed 38 innovative experiences that are already applying Artificial Intelligence to improve educational quality.

This activity not only consolidated interest in innovation but also provided the perfect finale to the 2024-2025 General Teaching Staff Training Plan, which has featured nearly 100 training activities, spread across 23 centres, with the participation of more than 1,500 lecturers from the institution.

In view of this warm reception, the University has confirmed that the 2nd Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with AI will be held in September 2026, with the aim of continuing to grow the number of teaching experiences shared.

What was the 1st Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with Artificial Intelligence?

The University of Salamanca invited the teaching community to the “1st Meeting on Good Teaching Practices with Artificial Intelligence”, held on 26 September 2025 at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca (Canalejas Campus). This event, part of the institution's Teacher Training Plan, was a unique opportunity to explore and share experiences in integrating AI into university teaching.

The main aim of the meeting was to encourage the exchange of knowledge and reflection on how AI can enrich teaching practice: a space where teaching staff were able to present and analyse success stories, learn about the latest tools and trends, and collaborate on the development of innovative projects.

The event also addressed the challenges and opportunities that AI poses in higher education, including ethical and pedagogical aspects, with the aim of inspiring teaching staff to experiment with new teaching strategies and to harness the potential of AI to improve students' learning experience.

Programme — Friday 26 September 2025

09:10–09:30 · Opening ceremony. Ms Berta María Gutiérrez Rodilla (Vice-Rector for Undergraduate Studies and Quality), Mr Ricardo Canal Bedia (Dean of the Faculty of Education), Ms Susana Olmos Migueláñez (Director of the IUCE) and Mr Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos (Training Coordinator).

09:30–10:30 · Keynote talk. “Teachers facing AI: evolution, training and concerns”, by Miguel Ángel Hernández Rodríguez (University of Salamanca). Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Education, Canalejas Campus.

10:30–11:15 · Debate on the future of AI in university teaching. Chair: Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos. Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Education.

11:15 · Break.

11:45–13:45 · Parallel sessions on good practices with AI. Group A — Degree Hall (ground floor, Solís Building); Group B — Room 16B (first floor, Cossío Building); Group C — Assembly Hall (ground floor, Cossío Building); Group D — Room 22B (second floor, Cossío Building).

11:45–13:45 · Informal meeting point. In Room 6A (ground floor, Solís Building), a space for debating, comparing ideas and forming working groups on the future of AI. Chair: Alberto Ortiz López.

Queries about the training programme are handled at coord.docencia@usal.es.

excerptEn: From 9 to 12 September 2025, the ECER International Conference of the European Educational Research Association (EERA) took place in Belgrade (Serbia), across several venues of the University of Belgrade, under the theme “Charting the Way Forward: Education, Research, Potentials and Perspectives”. Professors Fernando Martínez Abad and Juan Pablo Hernández…
contentEn:
From 9 to 12 September 2025, the ECER International Conference of the European Educational Research Association (EERA) took place in Belgrade (Serbia), across several venues of the University of Belgrade, under the theme “Charting the Way Forward: Education, Research, Potentials and Perspectives”. 

Professors Fernando Martínez Abad and Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos attended to present to the education community results arising from the project: Assessment and promotion of educational equity in secondary education (EVIDENCE): secondary analysis of the PISA assessments and training of key educational agents.

Likewise, María Holgado Aguadero, a student on the University of Salamanca's “Education in the Knowledge Society” Doctoral Programme, attended the international gathering to present progress on her doctoral thesis study linked to the project.

Among the countless papers presented, the Salamanca researchers contributed relevant results on educational equity in an international context.

Notably, ECER 2028 will be held at the IUCE, which expects to welcome some 3,000 researchers in the field of education.

titleEn: Participation in the 11th IEA International Research Conference (IEA IRC)
excerptEn: From 25 to 27 June 2025, the 11th IEA International Research Conference (IEA IRC) of the International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement (IEA) took place in Rome, at the Department of Humanities of Roma Tre University, devoted to the theme “Research on education for sustainable futures”. The students of the…
contentEn: From 25 to 27 June 2025, the 11th IEA International Research Conference (IEA IRC) of the International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement (IEA) took place in Rome, at the Department of Humanities of Roma Tre University, devoted to the theme “Research on education for sustainable futures”. Inés García Bohórquez and Marina García Herrero, students on the University of Salamanca's “Education in the Knowledge Society” Doctoral Programme, together with Professors María José Rodríguez Conde, Camilo Ruiz Méndez and Fernando Martínez Abad, were the only Spanish representatives at the entire conference.

titleEn: Success of the creative workshop “Printed Garden: botanical transfer between art and heritage”
excerptEn: On 15 and 16 May, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) hosted the creative workshop Printed Garden: a botanical transfer workshop, an educational initiative linked to the exhibition Flor en un jardín de sueños and led by the artists and teachers Alfredo Omaña and Carmen López-San Segundo. The activity, held in the…
contentEn:
On 15 and 16 May, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) hosted the creative workshop Printed Garden: a botanical transfer workshop , an educational initiative linked to the exhibition Flor en un jardín de sueños and led by the artists and teachers Alfredo Omaña and Carmen López-San Segundo.

The activity, held in the Multi-Purpose Room of the Solís Building and in the classroom of the Department of Didactics of Plastic Expression of the University of Salamanca, brought together students, teachers and people interested in exploring new forms of dialogue between art, nature and heritage. Over two evening sessions, participants delved into the transfer technique, an artistic process that makes it possible to move images onto different supports — such as fabric, wood, glass or ceramics — experimenting with compositions inspired by the botanical richness of the exhibition.

The workshop offered a hands-on, sensory experience, inviting reflection on the creative possibilities of natural heritage and its reinterpretation through contemporary art. Through images of plants and flowers, attendees discovered how natural forms can take on new life on different materials, generating unique works that converse with tradition and innovation.

The initiative forms part of the parallel activities of the exhibition Flor en un jardín de sueños and reinforces the IUCE's commitment to the creative and participatory dissemination of cultural and scientific heritage.

The Printed Garden workshop will be held again tomorrow and the day after (22 and 23 May), at the same time and place.

Further information at: https://causal.usal.es/jardin-impreso-taller-creativo-de-transferencia-botanica/

titleEn: Research stay of Professor Paola Perochena González at the IUCE
excerptEn: Over the past month, Dr Paola Perochena González has been on a research stay at the IUCE. She returns to her roots after some 15 years working at various public and private universities. Currently a permanent lecturer at the UNED, she has been mentored by Dr María José Rodríguez Conde, who…
contentEn:
Over the past month, Dr Paola Perochena González has been on a research stay at the IUCE. She returns to her roots after some 15 years working at various public and private universities. Currently a permanent lecturer at the UNED, she has been mentored by Dr María José Rodríguez Conde, who had supervised her doctoral thesis years earlier at our university.

In the day-to-day of this personal encounter, several activities took place, some arising spontaneously and others planned in advance.

On 27 March she gave the lecture entitled “Teaching innovation through Service-Learning: an online experience”.  It was aimed at all interested staff of the Institute and of the Faculties of Education of our University. This is one of the strands of work she carries out as part of the UNED's consolidated Innovation Group COETIC (Teaching Innovation Group for the development of ethical and civic competence and community-based methodologies). The lecture focused on a project with more than 8 years' history entitled “Español en vivo” (EsVivo) which, based on the APSv methodology (virtual service-learning), fosters the development of soft skills among its participants. The slides from this lecture are available at this link: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15210916

On 7 April she also delivered a seminar entitled “Publishing an SLR using Spanish databases: challenges, opportunities and lessons learned” within the “Education in the Knowledge Society Doctoral Programme”. In the words of the attendees, the seminar proved valuable, useful, practical and enjoyable.

The exchanges in informal meetings also proved highly enriching. It was time very well spent which, among other publications, will most immediately result in a contribution to the forthcoming XVIII National and XI Ibero-American Congress of Pedagogy (SEPCON25). The co-supervision of a doctoral thesis is also planned.

From this experience, the professor takes away a great deal of learning and the enjoyment of a month of exchange, collaboration, new relationships and the strengthening of existing ones. The IUCE thanks her for her dedication and professionalism during her stay and looks forward to counting on her collaboration in future actions.

titleEn: SEMINAR HACKING HERITAGE COMMUNICATION: IMMERSIVE STORYTELLING TO FEEL ART, SCIENCE AND HISTORY
excerptEn: On Wednesday 26 March, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) organised the seminar-workshop Hacking Heritage Communication: Immersive Storytelling to Feel Art, Science and History, delivered by António Baía Reis. The activity, which took place in the Solís Building of the University of Salamanca, offered a…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 26 March, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) organised the seminar-workshop Hacking Heritage Communication: Immersive Storytelling to Feel Art, Science and History, delivered by António Baía Reis. The activity, which took place in the Solís Building of the University of Salamanca, offered a searching reflection on the limitations of conventional models of outreach and presented cases of immersive storytelling applied to art, science and heritage.
The day was divided into two parts: a hands-on experience and an interactive seminar-workshop. The hands-on experience, held in the University's Sala de la Columna, allowed participants to visit the exhibition Flor en un Jardín de Sueños, where they analysed a real case of immersive storytelling applied to scientific heritage.


The seminar-workshop that followed, from 19:00 to 21:00, was a space for collective reflection discussing new ways of communicating heritage through emerging technologies, such as virtual (VR), augmented (AR) and mixed reality (MR), as well as the use of multisensory narratives. António Baía Reis shared examples of immersive experiences that are changing the way art, science and history are presented and experienced by the public.


The activity was aimed at students, teachers, creators, researchers and anyone interested in heritage and art communication. Throughout the seminar, attendees were able to reflect on how immersive narratives and emerging technologies can create a deeper emotional connection with the public, making outreach not only informative but also transformative.


Further information on forthcoming activities at: https://causal.usal.es/conferencias-cursos-y-seminarios/

titleEn: Workshop on Freinet Pedagogy for future teachers
excerptEn: On Friday 14 March, in the IUCE classrooms, teachers from the MCEP of Salamanca ran a training workshop to bring Freinet Pedagogy to students. In the words of Juan Fernández Platero (MCEP Salamanca), the driving force behind the activity together with several lecturers from the Faculty of Education…
contentEn:
On Friday 14 March, in the IUCE classrooms, teachers from the MCEP of Salamanca ran a training workshop to bring Freinet Pedagogy to students.

In the words of Juan Fernández Platero (MCEP Salamanca), the driving force behind the activity together with several lecturers from the Faculty of Education: we wanted to create an atmosphere of trust, in the spirit of our MCEP Body Workshop, so we gradually introduced games, dances, laughter and close interaction. We know that the body predisposes us. We talked about our way of seeing school, about the Natural Method and Freinet techniques. With Edgar Morin, we believe that “method does not precede action but emerges from it”. We also spoke about Emancipation — there is no humanity without a critical spirit, without social commitment  — and above all, we stressed the theme of  cooperation. We quoted Emmanuel Lebina: it is, as he says, about empathy understood as  “the mutual recognition  of humanity”

It is about realising, becoming aware, that quality cooperation is the kind that seeks the common good, the kind that aims for everyone to move forward and, to that end, takes care to help and to look after others. Realising, becoming aware, that this kind of cooperation is the goal, because such cooperation is a tool, a path,  the practical embodiment of  Emancipation.

Throughout the  workshop, Juan Fernández explains, we recommend paying attention, during play and  when we cooperate, to the characteristics of that relationship; if we do this we will make progress in discovering the keys to quality cooperation. The activity concludes with creative dramatisation  in small groups as the logical culmination of the workshop, demonstrating how the body contributes to quality cooperation.

“It was lovely while it lasted. We intend to keep doing things at the university because we want Freinet Pedagogy to have an ever greater presence in schools,” concludes the driving force behind the activity.

Without a doubt, given the level of satisfaction among attendees, this will be the first of many similar experiences bringing Freinet Pedagogy closer to future education professionals.
